name: Broccoli, Cauliflower & Carrots with Parmesan Bread Crumbs
recipeIngredient: 1 pound carrots, peeled and cut into 1/4-inch diagonal slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim broccoli, reserving stems for another use, and cut florets into 1-inch pieces (about 5 cups).
2. Trim cauliflower and cut florets into 1-inch pieces (about 5 cups).
3. In a large saucepan of boiling salted water, cook vegetables until crisp-tender, 2 to 5 minutes.
4. Drain vegetables in a colander and refresh under cold water to stop cooking; drain well.
- Vegetables may be prepared up to this point 1 day ahead and chilled, covered.
5. In a large heavy skillet, heat oil and 2 tablespoons butter over moderately high heat until foam begins to subside.
6. Add bread crumbs and sauté, stirring, until golden (about 3-4 minutes).
7. Stir in Parmesan, cayenne, and salt to taste; continue sautéing, stirring, until crisp.
- Bread crumbs may be prepared up to 3 days ahead and kept in an airtight container.
8. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
9. In the same skillet, melt the remaining tablespoon butter over moderate heat and toss vegetables with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Sprinkle vegetables with bread crumbs and toss to combine.
11. Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake, uncovered, for 10 minutes, or until just heated through.
12. Yield: 8 servings
